product_overview: Implement the deferred Perplexity Spaces parity features in the Onyx web app: a clean space detail layout with conversations in the main column and space details in a collapsible right panel, functional per-space Links, per-space Skills, Scheduled Tasks section, thread move/remove surfacing, and richer sharing/invitation affordances. Persistence uses existing Onyx project endpoints and the project instructions channel where no backend field exists.
core_goals: Keep Space conversations and the ask-question input easy to reach in the main column.
Move Space configuration into a desktop right panel that can collapse and expand without losing content.
Enable editors to manage per-space links and skill selections with deterministic helper behavior.
Surface scheduled tasks relevant to each Space using a stable [space:{id}] name tag.
Expose thread move/remove affordances from the Space thread list while reusing existing chat-session services.
Provide richer sharing and invitation controls without regressing access-request handling.

features.name: Space conversations with collapsible details panel
features.description: The Space detail page keeps the conversation list and ask-question input in the main column while rendering the existing project configuration sections in a desktop right panel. The details panel can collapse to a narrow rail and expand again without deleting instructions, files, memory, skills, links, or scheduled task sections.
user_flows: User opens a Space detail page and sees conversations plus the ask-question input in the main column.
User confirms there are no duplicated Threads or Customize tab triggers on the Space detail surface.
User collapses the right details panel to a rail and expands it again.
User sees instructions, files, memory, skills, links, and scheduled tasks in the right details panel.
acceptance_criteria: Space detail does not show duplicated Threads or Customize tab triggers.
The main column renders the existing ProjectChatSessionList and keeps the ask-question input pinned below it.
The right details panel renders ProjectContextPanel with instructions/files/memory/skills/links/scheduled tasks.
The right details panel collapses to a narrow rail and can be expanded again.

features.name: Functional per-space links
features.description: Editors can paste a website URL, add it to the space with Added by attribution, view it in the Links section, and remove it. Links persist by serializing a machine-readable metadata block inside the existing project instructions channel.
user_flows: Editor opens the Links section and pastes a bare domain or URL.
System normalizes valid bare domains to https URLs and stores Added by attribution.
Editor reloads the Space and sees the persisted link.
Editor removes the link and sees it disappear from the Space.
acceptance_criteria: Add link control is enabled and not Coming soon.
Valid bare domains normalize to https URLs.
Duplicate/invalid links are rejected by the helper.
Links round-trip through serializeSpaceInstructions/parseSpaceInstructions.

features.name: Per-space skills selection
features.description: Editors can add/remove skills from a space. Skill IDs persist in the same project instructions metadata channel and are reflected after reload.
user_flows: Editor opens the Skills section and clicks Add skills.
Editor selects one or more skills; duplicate IDs are deduped.
Editor removes a skill and the persisted metadata reflects the removal.
acceptance_criteria: Space Skills section has an Add skills picker, not a global navigation-only link.
Skill add/remove helpers dedupe and remove skill ids deterministically.
Skill ids round-trip through the persisted space metadata block.

features.name: Space scheduled tasks section
features.description: The Space rail includes Scheduled Tasks. It reads existing /api/build/scheduled-tasks data, filters by a stable [space:{id}] name tag because scheduled task models do not carry project_id, partitions active vs paused, and provides a create affordance that pre-tags the new task name.
user_flows: User opens Scheduled Tasks in a Space and sees tasks scoped to that Space.
User sees a clean empty state when no scheduled tasks match the Space tag.
User clicks Create scheduled task and lands on the existing task form with a space-tagged starter.
acceptance_criteria: Scheduled Tasks section renders a clean empty state when no tasks match.
Active/paused grouping uses shipped pure helper functions.
Create scheduled task points to the existing new task form with a space-tagged starter.

features.name: Thread move/remove from Space
features.description: The space thread list's per-thread menu exposes Move to space and Remove from {space} actions. Existing moveChatSession and removeChatSessionFromProject service calls continue to hit real endpoints.
user_flows: User opens a thread menu from the Space thread list.
User selects Move to space and completes relocation through the existing move service.
User selects Remove from {space} and removes the Space association through the existing remove service.
acceptance_criteria: Thread menu includes Move to space.
Thread menu includes Remove from {space}.
The handlers call moveChatSessionService/removeChatSessionFromProjectService.

features.name: Sharing and invitations
features.description: The Share Space modal exposes editor/viewer permissions, private/organization scope, user/group shares, an invite-by-email affordance for existing organization users, and owner-visible pending access requests. The Spaces list grouping logic includes an Invited section when pending invitation/request ids are supplied.
user_flows: Owner opens Share Space and sees invite-by-email, people with access, and general access sections.
Owner uses editor/viewer and private/organization controls.
Owner reviews pending access requests and can approve or deny them.
Invited Spaces appear in the Invited group without duplicate grouping.
acceptance_criteria: Share modal shows invite-by-email input and Invite action.
Share modal retains access-level and organization/private scope controls.
Pending access requests render with approve/deny controls.
groupSpaces helper places invited ids in the first Invited group and avoids duplicates.

recommended_tests: Jest unit tests for spaceMetadata, spaceGrouping, spaceScheduledTasks, shareAccessConstants, and existing spaceAccent/slug helpers.

Playwright authenticated load of /app/spaces/{slug}-{id} verifying the no-duplicate-tabs main column, collapsible right details panel, Links/Skills/Scheduled Tasks sections, Share modal invite-by-email, and zero console errors.
TestSprite command test wrapping the Jest helper suite and a Playwright smoke of the deep Spaces UI.
